expectations.text: Has [MenuItem] attribute on the start method
expectations.evidence: Line 53: `[MenuItem("Tools/Frame Debugger/Capture All Events")]` on `public static void StartCapture()`

expectations.text: Uses EditorApplication.update as the capture loop tick mechanism
expectations.evidence: Line 83: `EditorApplication.update += CaptureTick;` subscribes the handler; Line 268: `EditorApplication.update -= CaptureTick;` removes it in Cleanup

expectations.text: Sets FrameDebuggerReflect.SetLimit per event iteration (limit = targetIndex + 1 pattern)
Has three distinct phases in the update loop: wait for events, iterate with limit, cleanup on completion
expectations.evidence: Lines 25-33: `Phase` enum with `WaitingForEvents`, `Capturing`, `Done`; Lines 97-109: switch statement dispatching to `TickWaitForEvents()`, `TickCaptureEvent()`, and `Cleanup()`

expectations.text: Writes CSV output with headers including shaderName/passName/lightMode/passIndex/shaderKeywords columns
Removes EditorApplication.update handler and disables FrameDebugger in completion/cleanup path
expectations.evidence: Lines 267-281: `Cleanup()` removes delegate (`EditorApplication.update -= CaptureTick`) and disables FrameDebugger (`FrameDebuggerReflect.SetEnabled(false, ...)`)

expectations.text: Has proper CSV escaping for special characters (commas, quotes, newlines)
expectations.evidence: Lines 311-322: `CsvEscape()` implements RFC 4180 quoting for commas, double-quotes, `\n`, `\r`; Line 327: `SpecialCsvChars = { ',', '"', '\n', '\r' }`

expectations.text: Calls RepaintAllViews or equivalent when event count is 0 to trigger event population

eval_feedback.overall: All 8 expectations are fully met. The generated code is well-structured with a clean three-phase state machine, proper CSV escaping per RFC 4180, correct use of EditorApplication.update for the capture loop, and thorough cleanup logic.
